title: Purely Roasted Tunisian Lamb Casserole
description: A creamy and flavorful Tunisian dish featuring roasted lamb and crusty bread, perfect for a crowd, this recipe is a low-fat friendly version of a classic en-croute casserole.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the lamb shoulder with salt and pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the lamb shoulder until browned on all sides, then remove from the pot.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for an additional minute.
Stir in the tomato paste, cumin, and paprika, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the browned lamb shoulder back to the pot, cover, and transfer to the preheated oven.
Roast the lamb for 20 minutes, then reduce the oven temperature to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Continue roasting the lamb for an additional 20 minutes, or until tender.
Slice the crusty bread into 1-inch thick slices and toast until lightly browned.
Assemble the casserole by placing a layer of toasted bread in the bottom of a 9x13 inch baking dish, followed by a layer of the roasted lamb and sauce, and finally a layer of low-fat cream.
